dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.3 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Google Sheets write tools — closes #10. Three new tools turn an ad-hoc data structure (a markdown table from a GitHub issue, a CSV scrape) into a real spreadsheet with addressable cells, not just an uploaded blob: cb_gsheets_create_spreadsheet, cb_gsheets_update_values, cb_gsheets_append_values. The Google OAuth scope set adds `spreadsheets` — existing v11.x users must Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector once to upgrade the token. Disconnect now keeps your saved credentials. Click Disconnect in Settings → Local MCP and the Keychain payload stays put — only the Enabled flag flips off. The button next to the row swaps from Disconnect to Reconnect, which verifies the saved token against the provider (Slack auth.test / ClickUp /user / GitHub /user / GDrive /about with refresh) and flips Enabled back on if still valid. No re-paste, no re-OAuth. Replace + Forget. Replace… opens the paste-token / OAuth sheet directly while a connector is still enabled — useful for rotating a token without disconnecting first. Forget (trash icon) hard-deletes the Keychain payload AND the registry entry; the next Connect must enter from scratch. Use Forget for security rotation, Disconnect for everyday "pause this for a bit".

Existing v11.x Google tokens are missing the `spreadsheets` scope. The cb_gsheets_* tools will return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do. Reconnect is not wired for GitLab (multi-instance + per-instance base URL) or Bitwarden (no verify API). Their Reconnect button takes you straight to the existing add-instance / paste-token sheet.
